claims.claim: The denial is administrative (AD-01, documentation not received) rather than a merits denial and is curable by supplying the record.
Section 4 of MP-RAD-014 requires documentation of symptom duration, conservative therapy modalities and duration, objective exam findings, and the clinical question.
The chart documents symptom duration of >6 years (chronic LBP onset 2019-10-30).
The chart documents a supervised movement therapy course of nine sessions (2019-11-05 to 2019-12-13) as conservative therapy.
The missing documentation is now supplied and the administrative gap is closed.
Policy Section 2.1 deems lumbar MRI medically necessary for LBP ≥6 weeks that failed a documented trial of conservative therapy (PT, NSAID/analgesic, or supervised home exercise).
The member satisfies all elements of Section 2.1 (duration, documented conservative trial, and failure of that trial).
Failure of conservative therapy is established because LBP remains an active chronic problem years later.
Section 2.1 does not require objective neurologic findings (those belong to criterion 2.3).
The appeal is timely, filed well within the 2027-01-10 deadline.

missing_elements: Member date of birth is null/absent — a common identifier payers require for matching.
No citation/attachment reference for the actual chart records; all patient-specific facts (onset date, therapy sessions) are asserted without an evidence exhibit in the citation list.
Appeal level is stated as 'First-Level' but denial's appeal_level is null — unconfirmed.

Letter is signed by 'Patient Access Team,' not by or with an explicit signature/attestation from the ordering physician; payer may require provider signature or member authorization for representation.

needs_human.issue: Conservative therapy and symptom-onset evidence is from 2019 (6+ years old). A payer will likely reject Section 2.1 satisfaction because there is no recent/current conservative therapy trial documented — this is a substantive merits vulnerability, not merely administrative.

The letter itself concedes two of four Section 4 items (objective exam findings and the clinical question) are NOT documented/supplied, so the 'administrative cure' is incomplete and the payer can deny again for the same reason.
'Movement therapy' is characterized as qualifying supervised conservative therapy / PT-equivalent under 2.1 — verify the chart actually supports this classification and duration.
Confirm the patient chart is actually attached and that specific quoted entries match the record, since none are backed by a citation.
Add member DOB and confirm correct appeal level and required signature/authorization before sending.

summary: The appeal is timely (176 days before the 2027-01-10 deadline) and correctly frames the AD-01 denial as a curable documentation issue with accurate policy citations. However, it has serious substantive weaknesses: the conservative therapy and symptom-onset evidence is over six years old with no current trial, the letter openly concedes that two of the four required Section 4 items are still missing, and all patient-specific facts are asserted without any chart citation or confirmed attachment. A human must verify the chart attachment, address the stale/incomplete clinical evidence, and add missing identifiers/signature before this can go out.
